hero.heading: Virtual Care
hero.subhead: Evenings and weekends, a Vero Beach Adult Medicine doctor is still available to you.

intro.heading: Still your
intro.body1: When your concern can't wait, a Vero Beach Adult Medicine doctor is available to you — not a call center, not a stranger. Your on-call physician can speak with you and, if appropriate, see you virtually through Privia Virtual Clinic.
intro.body2: Same practice. Same doctors. Just different hours.
intro.costNote: Virtual visits are billed separately and may have a co-pay. Have your insurance card handy. Text or call us with billing questions.
intro.callLabel: Need Help? Text or Call Us

setup.heading: Two minutes of prep
setup.intro: The most common reason a virtual visit doesn't work is a camera or microphone blocked by your device. Here's how to clear it before you join.

mobile.label: A Phone
steps: Download the Privia Health app (App Store or Google Play), or open your visit link in your mobile browser.
Sign in to your Privia account before the visit starts.
When your phone asks for camera and microphone access, tap Allow.
Use Wi-Fi if possible — video calls are more stable than cellular.
If the call drops, check your confirmation text or email for a rejoin link. Your doctor may also call you back directly.
mobile.permissionNote: Tap Allow — you'll see a prompt like this.

desktop.label: A Computer
steps: Use Google Chrome if possible — it's Privia's recommended browser. Edge and Safari also work. Internet Explorer is not supported.
When your browser asks for camera and microphone access, click Allow.
If you see a camera icon with a line through it in the address bar, click it to change the permission to Allow.
Close Zoom, FaceTime, Teams, or any other app currently using your camera.
If the call drops, check your confirmation email or text for a rejoin link. Your doctor may also call you back directly.
desktop.permissionNote: Click Allow — you'll see a prompt like this.

items.label: Insurance & medications
items.body: Have your insurance card and a current medication list nearby before you join.

items.label: Stay charged
items.body: Plug in your device or confirm your battery is above 20% before the visit begins.

items.label: Strong connection
items.body: Privia requires at least 20 Mbps. Not sure? Test at speedtest.net before your visit. If Wi-Fi is slow, try moving closer to your router or switching to a wired connection.

items.label: Good lighting
items.body: Face a window or a lamp so the provider can see you clearly. Avoid sitting with a bright light behind you.

items.label: Be ready when the call comes
items.body: Your doctor will call you when they're ready. Have your device open, camera and microphone allowed, and a quiet spot picked out before you expect the call.

items.label: Open a fresh window
items.body: Open your virtual visit in its own browser window and close extra tabs. Other open windows can compete for bandwidth and slow the connection.

helpCta.heading: We're a message
helpCta.body: If you run into technical difficulties or have questions before your virtual visit, text or call our office — we'll help you get connected.
